memes.label: "Front Matter"
memes.description: "Title page and table of contents for A Strange Manuscript Found in a Copper Cylinder by James De Mille"

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "Adam More"
memes.description: "The English sailor and first-person narrator who discovers the Kosekin subterranean civilization after being swept beneath the Antarctic ice"

memes.label: Agnew
memes.description: Agnew, the second mate of the ship and Adam More's companion during the Antarctic drift — an optimist whose hope sustains both men through the early chapters before his death in the subterranean passage.

memes.label: "Almah"
memes.description: "A Kosekin woman who secretly loves life and light, becomes Adam More's beloved, and whose selection for the sacrificial ceremony drives the novel's central dramatic conflict between love and death."

memes.label: Amir
memes.description: The Amir — the central metropolis of the Kosekin, a vast subterranean city where ceremonies, feasts, sacrifices, and the seat of government are located.

memes.label: Antarctic Voyage
memes.description: The 1843 Antarctic expedition of the ship Trevelyan that led to Adam More and Agnew's discovery of the subterranean Kosekin world.

memes.label: "Athaleb"
memes.description: "The enormous flying reptile (pterodactyl-like creature) tamed by the Kosekin for aerial transport, which plays a pivotal role in the escape of Adam More and Layelah"

memes.label: Athaleb Class
memes.description: A species of giant flying reptile native to the Kosekin world, capable of carrying riders over long distances and migrating seasonally to Magones.

memes.label: Athon
memes.description: A Kosekin social rank title for wealthy capitalists and military officers, considered low-status under the Kosekin inversion of values.

memes.label: "Athon Class"
memes.description: "The wealthy class in Kosekin society — burdened by riches and desperate to give everything away, forming the rung above Meleks and Kohens on the inverted social ladder"

memes.label: "Blessing of Death"
memes.description: "The central theological doctrine of the Kosekin people — the belief that death is the supreme blessing and the greatest gift one can bestow upon another"

memes.label: Captain Bennet
memes.description: Captain Bennet — master of the ship Trevelyan, an old and cautious commander who opposed the fatal shore expedition of Adam More and Agnew.

memes.label: Cavern-Dwellers
memes.description: The Kosekin people as a race of underground cave-dwellers whose physiology, culture, and psychology are shaped by their subterranean habitat.

memes.label: Ceremony of Separation
memes.description: The Ceremony of Separation — the Kosekin ritual preceding the Day of Sacrifice, in which Almah formally separates from Adam More to save his life.

memes.label: Chief Pauper (Kosekin)
memes.description: The highest-ranking official in Kosekin society — the Chief Pauper, whose squalor, poverty, and office of sacrificial executioner mark the apex of Kosekin honor.

memes.label: Council of Paupers (Kosekin)
memes.description: The Council of Paupers — the supreme governing body of the Kosekin, composed of the most honored paupers who deliberate in darkness and control all state affairs.

memes.label: Dark Season
memes.description: The six-month period of continuous darkness in the subterranean Kosekin world, marked by intensified ceremonial activity and culminating in the great sacrifice.

memes.label: "Darkness and Light"
memes.description: "The symbolic cosmology of the Kosekin people, who revere darkness and abhor light, inverting the universal human preference for illumination"

memes.label: The Day of Sacrifice
memes.description: The Day of Sacrifice — the climactic sacrificial ceremony at the end of the dark season, where the most honored Kosekin receive the blessing of public death.

memes.label: "Death-Worship (Kosekin)"
memes.description: "The central religious and social tenet of Kosekin society: the passionate love of death as the highest blessing, driving every aspect of their inverted civilization"

memes.label: Desolation Island
memes.description: Desolation Island (the Kerguelen Islands) — the last known landmark sighted by the Trevelyan before Adam More's drift into the Antarctic unknown. Used by the frame-narrator characters to authenticate the manuscript.

memes.label: "Dr. Congreve"
memes.description: "The physician aboard the Falcon who approaches More's manuscript with scientific empiricism"

memes.label: "Frame Narrative"
memes.description: "The novel's distinctive narrative architecture: four Englishmen aboard a yacht reading and debating Adam More's found manuscript, creating a multi-layered framework of interpretation and skepticism"

memes.label: Gojin
memes.description: Broader ethnic and geographic category of non-Kosekin peoples in the subterranean world, of whom the Orin are a life-loving subgroup.

memes.label: "Grimm's Law (Kosekin)"
memes.description: "The philological principle applied by Melick and the yacht companions to demonstrate a linguistic connection between the Kosekin language and Hebrew, serving both narrative world-building and satirical purposes"

memes.label: "Inversion Principle"
memes.description: "The systematic inversion of Western values that governs every aspect of Kosekin society — the foundational satirical mechanism of De Mille's novel"

memes.label: Jom
memes.description: The Kosekin unit of time equivalent to a day, reflecting the linguistic distinctiveness of the subterranean culture.

memes.label: "Kohen Gadol"
memes.description: "The highest-ranking Kosekin official — the high priest and ruler who is paradoxically burdened with immense wealth and power as punishment rather than privilege"

memes.label: The Kohen Priesthood
memes.description: The religious hierarchy of Kosekin society, headed by the Kohen Gadol, that administers the sacrificial system and embodies the culture's inversion of values — combining priestly benevolence with ritualized killing.

memes.label: Kohen Religious Hierarchy
memes.description: The religious and social authority structure centered on the Kohen priesthood, which governs Kosekin society through control of ritual sacrifice and moral doctrine.

memes.label: Kosekin Architecture
memes.description: The architectural tradition of the Kosekin people, characterized by pyramidal-roofed stone edifices, cave dwellings carved into mountainsides, and monumental public structures.

memes.label: "Kosekin Cavern World"
memes.description: "The vast underground cavern system beneath the Antarctic ice that houses the Kosekin civilization and its prehistoric ecosystem"

memes.label: Kosekin Crimes
memes.description: The inverted Kosekin system of crime and punishment, in which giving wealth secretly, forcing oneself as a slave, and other acts of unselfishness are criminalized and punished with increased luxury.

memes.label: "Kosekin Culture"
memes.description: "The fictional underground civilization whose values invert those of 19th-century Western society, central to James De Mille's satirical novel"

memes.label: Kosekin Food and Subsistence
memes.description: The dietary customs, food sources, ceremonial feasting practices, and social significance of food distribution among the Kosekin people.

memes.label: "Kosekin Language"
memes.description: "The Semitic-based language of the Kosekin people, which Oxenden demonstrates is related to Hebrew through Grimm's Law consonant shifts, supporting the lost tribes theory"

memes.label: Kosekin Maritime Culture
memes.description: The maritime customs, vessels, and social organization of seafaring among the Kosekin people of the underground sea.

memes.label: Kosekin Pirates
memes.description: The Kosekin practice of maritime piracy as an expression of self-sacrifice, in which pirates forcibly transfer their own goods to others rather than seizing valuables from victims.

memes.label: Kosekin Poetry and Literature
memes.description: The poetic and literary tradition of the Kosekin people, in which paupers, street-sweepers, and unhappy love are celebrated as the highest aesthetic subjects.

memes.label: "Kosekin Prison System"
memes.description: "The inverted penal system of the Kosekin in which prisoners are punished with lavish splendor — palaces, retinues, and luxury — while 'freedom' is found in poverty"

memes.label: The Kosekin People
memes.description: Ethnographic overview of the Kosekin people — a subterranean race dwelling in Antarctic caverns, characterized by their love of darkness, death, and self-sacrifice.

memes.label: Kosekin Revenge
memes.description: The inverted Kosekin concept of revenge: a "sleepless and vehement desire to bestow some adequate and corresponding benefit" upon another, rather than to harm them.

memes.label: Kosekin Sacrifice Rituals
memes.description: The elaborate public sacrifice ceremonies of Kosekin society, in which victims are honored with the most coveted death and the entire community participates in ritual celebration of mortality.

memes.label: Kosekin Social Classes
memes.description: The inverted social hierarchy of the Kosekin, in which paupers form the highest and most envied class, while the wealthy — Athons, Meleks, and Kohens — occupy the lowest social ranks.

memes.label: "Kosekin Value System"
memes.description: "The complete moral inversion at the heart of Kosekin society, where darkness, death, poverty, and self-denial are worshiped while light, life, wealth, and self-interest are despised"

memes.label: "Layelah"
memes.description: "The Kohen Gadol's daughter who secretly rejects Kosekin values, masters the athaleb flying monster, and orchestrates the escape to the land of light — the novel's most radical and psychologically complex figure."

memes.label: "Light-Dark Inversion"
memes.description: "The Kosekin cosmological inversion in which darkness is sacred and light is profane, forming the symbolic foundation of their religious worldview and daily practices"

memes.description: Complete index of all concept pages in the wiki, dynamically generated from frontmatter data.

memes.label: Persons
memes.description: Complete index of all person/character pages in the wiki, dynamically generated from frontmatter data.

memes.label: Places
memes.description: Complete index of all place/location pages in the wiki, dynamically generated from frontmatter data.

memes.label: "Lord Featherstone"
memes.description: "Owner of the yacht Falcon, whose discovery of the copper cylinder initiates the frame narrative"

memes.label: Love and Death (Kosekin Inversion)
memes.description: The central Kosekin cultural inversion in which death is loved and sought as the highest blessing, while love itself becomes a path to the desired death.

memes.label: "Magones"
memes.description: "The volcanic Island of Fire in the subterranean sea — a desolate place of exile for honored paupers and the seasonal destination of the athaleb migration"

memes.label: Malca
memes.description: The Kosekin title for "queen," paradoxically the lowest social rank in the inverted Kosekin hierarchy. Held by Layelah.

memes.label: Melek
memes.description: A mid-tier Kosekin social rank for authors, scholars, and legislators, positioned between Athon and Kohen in the inverted Kosekin hierarchy.

memes.label: "Melick"
memes.description: "The skeptical wit among the frame-narrative gentlemen, folding paper boats and questioning everything"

memes.label: "Mista Kosek"
memes.description: "The central ceremonial ritual of the Kosekin people, a human sacrifice feast celebrating darkness and death"

memes.label: Opkuk
memes.description: A giant flightless bird native to the Kosekin subterranean world, described as larger than an ostrich and used for transport and hauling heavy loads.

memes.label: Opmahera
memes.description: An enormous flightless bird of the Kosekin subterranean world, larger than the opkuk and "as tall as giraffes," used primarily as a riding mount.

memes.label: Orin
memes.description: A distinct people among the Gojin who love life and light, inverting Kosekin death-worship, and whose land is a key destination in the escape narrative.

memes.label: "Oxenden"
memes.description: "Featherstone's intellectual friend who develops the linguistic theory connecting Kosekin to Hebrew through Grimm's Law"

memes.label: "Poverty as Virtue"
memes.description: "The Kosekin economic value system in which poverty is the highest good, wealth a curse, and people compete to give away rather than accumulate possessions"

memes.label: "Sacred Hunt"
memes.description: "The ritual maritime hunt in which Kosekin warriors attack sea-monsters (jantannin) with the aim of being killed — gaining the blessing of death through glorious sacrifice"

memes.label: Self-Sacrifice (Kosekin)
memes.description: The Kosekin ideal of self-sacrifice: the passionate drive to lay down one's life for others, rooted in the society's fundamental love of death and contempt for life.

memes.label: Semitic Theory of Kosekin Origins
memes.description: Oxenden and Melick's scholarly theory in the frame narrative that the Kosekin are a Semitic people, based on linguistic, physical, and cultural evidence.

memes.label: "Ceremony of Separation"
memes.description: "The Kosekin religious and legal ceremony that formally dissolves a romantic union, celebrated as a blessing since separation is valued over togetherness in the inverted Kosekin value system"

memes.label: South Pole
memes.description: The geographical setting of the novel — a warm, habitable region at the South Pole leading to a vast subterranean world.

memes.label: "Subterranean Ecosystem"
memes.description: "The vast, volcanically-warmed cavern system beneath the Antarctic ice that hosts prehistoric flora and fauna alongside the Kosekin civilization"

memes.label: Epet (Kosekin Title)
memes.description: The Kosekin honorific title "Epet," meaning servant — one of the highest social distinctions, reflecting the inversion of Western status hierarchies.

memes.label: The Great Escape
memes.description: The dramatic flight of Adam More, Almah, and Layelah from the Kosekin cavern world aboard the athaleb — the novel's climactic action sequence that transforms the satirical travelogue into an adventure narrative.

memes.label: The Kohen
memes.description: The Kohen — a chief priest figure of the Kosekin, central antagonist who presides over the sacred hunt and the Mista Kosek sacrificial ceremony, representing the institutional face of Kosekin death-worship.

memes.label: Troglodytes
memes.description: Classical reference used by Oxenden in the frame narrative as a comparative anthropological link between the ancient cave-dwelling Troglodytes and the Kosekin.

memes.label: The Underground Sea
memes.description: The vast subterranean ocean beneath the Antarctic, fed by a polar maelstrom, across which Adam More voyages into the Kosekin cavern world — the novel's central geographic transition from surface to underworld.

memes.label: Volcanic Underground World
memes.description: The volcanic subterranean world at the South Pole — a vast, warm, habitable basin reached through a channel beneath the Antarctic mountains, featuring eternal twilight, a subterranean sea, and tropical climate.

memes.label: Wealth-Poverty Inversion (Kosekin)
memes.description: The Kosekin economic inversion in which poverty is the highest honor and wealth the greatest curse — a complete reversal of Western values.

memes.label: "World of Darkness"
memes.description: "The Kosekin conception of the afterlife — a realm of eternal darkness and bliss that the faithful enter through death, equated by the narrator with nothingness"
